summary: Simon Späti shares his personal journey of leaving the conventional 'Default Path' of career progression to forge his own 'Pathless Path' - a concept from Paul Millerd about embracing uncertainty over conformity. He traces how his supportive childhood, early passion for computers, sabbaticals in Australia and Bali, and relocating to Copenhagen shaped his identity. Along the way, he discovered stoicism as a guiding philosophy, fell in love with English as a creative medium, and ultimately found his life partner. The post concludes with his return to Switzerland, starting a family, and embracing remote work and writing - demonstrating that everyone's pathless path looks different.
key_points: A supportive childhood with unconditional love provides the foundation for taking risks later in life
Sabbaticals and mini-retirements at various life stages (not just at 65) offer crucial clarity and self-discovery
Learning English opened access to a world of knowledge and ultimately led to a passion for writing
Stoicism serves as a practical philosophy for navigating uncertainty and following intuition
The 'Pathless Path' isn't about rejecting all defaults - it's about consciously choosing your path rather than following a script
money_quotes: I have learned that some of my best decision-making comes from following my gut, heart, and intuition - a place of inner knowing.
The Pathless Path is experimenting on your own to find a life you want to live vs 'getting ahead' on a script others follow.
In these 'stress' situations, you get to know yourself and deeply engage with the surroundings and people around you.
Being a minimalist, I make impactful decisions. Saying no to things I didn't value meant I could follow my passions.
Ultimately, it is anyone's path; This was my Pathless Path, and it was, in a sense, genuinely pathless—following curiosity and my instincts.
Try to be happy with less. Try minimalism. Then, happiness = reality - expectations.
Finding love is likely to happen when you are your best self. Not when stuck in a daily commute or working 9-5.
key_insight: The most meaningful life decisions come from training your intuition through experience and reflection, then having the courage to follow it even when it diverges from conventional expectations.
